meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve Can't Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client builds. Learn advanced SaaS pricing strategies to finish projects faster using Antigravity and Claude Code. A pro-level checklist.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is often 'scope creep' disguised as low-value pricing. By implementing tiered SaaS pricing, you force clients to prioritize features, limit scope, and align incentives. Use automated toolchains like Antigravity and Claude Code to standardize delivery, ensuring you ship high-margin, production-ready code every single time.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a half-finished dashboard at 3:00 AM, the client is asking for 'one more feature,' and your profit margin is evaporating into the ether. This isn't a coding problem; it's a structural failure in how you value your output. When projects drag on, it’s because the price didn’t reflect the complexity, and the scope was never anchored to a business outcome.

It’s time to stop being a feature factory and start being a SaaS architect. By utilizing high-leverage tools like Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for autonomous logic implementation, you can reclaim your time. This checklist provides the exact framework to price your projects for profit and execute them with machine-like efficiency.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Stop charging by the hour. Break your build into three tiers: MVP, Growth, and Scale. This anchors the client's budget and prevents scope bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project requirements and generate a 3-tier pricing strategy. Tier 1: MVP (core functionality), Tier 2: Growth (added integrations), Tier 3: Scale (full automation). Output as a JSON schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a tiered project scope document based on the provided requirements.yaml file.'

steps.title: Automate Feature Scoping
steps.description: Use AI to enforce strict boundaries on what constitutes an MVP. If it's not in the tier, it's not in the build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a 'Scope Guard' policy that restricts feature additions unless they are explicitly priced as Change Orders.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review the current codebase against the MVP scope and flag any features that exceed the initial requirements.'

steps.title: Standardize the Boilerplate
steps.description: Never start from scratch. Use Antigravity to spin up a production-ready stack that you can deploy instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a SaaS boilerplate with Stripe integration, authentication, and database schema, optimized for rapid deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project structure using the standard SaaS boilerplate template.'

steps.title: Implement Pricing Logic
steps.description: Hardcode your pricing tiers into the application logic to allow self-service upgrades, reducing your management overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the Stripe subscription logic for three pricing tiers, including middleware to gate features based on the active plan.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement Stripe checkout sessions and feature-gating middleware for the existing project.'

steps.title: Continuous Delivery Loop
steps.description: Use Claude Code to run automated tests and deployment scripts after every feature completion to ensure you never have a 'big bang' release failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a CI/CD pipeline configuration that runs unit tests and deploys to staging upon every commit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Set up GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment.'

pro_tips: Always include a 20% 'discovery buffer' in your flat fee; if the project finishes early, that’s your efficiency bonus.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation as you build; clients perceive higher value in well-documented systems.
Force clients to sign off on the 'Scope Guard' document before you write a single line of code.
Implement a 'sunset clause' in your pricing: if a project goes beyond the agreed date due to client delays, the price increases by 15%.
Automate your payment collection via Stripe Webhooks so you never have to manually invoice for milestones.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully implemented your first tiered build, audit your last three projects to identify the exact point where scope creep occurred. Apply the 'Scope Guard' policy to all future contracts to maintain your profit margins.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to stop the cycle of unfinished projects and start shipping profitable SaaS products. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
